subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Chemical Splash to the Eyes
description: One-minute skill: Chemical Splash to the Eyes.

hook.text: A chemical splash to the eye can cause blindness in minutes. The first 10 seconds are critical.

buildup.text: Alkalis like drain cleaner penetrate deeper and faster than acids, causing worse damage.

discovery.text: Immediate flushing with water for at least 20 minutes is the single most important treatment.

twist.text: People instinctively close their eyes tight—but you must hold them open and flush continuously.

climax.text: Emergency eyewash stations in workplaces must reach the eyes within 10 seconds of exposure.

punchline.text: Don't think. Flush. Twenty minutes of water beats any eye drop ever made.

quiz.question: How long should you flush an eye after a chemical splash?
